# google_chronicle_parser_extension

A parser extension allows customers to extend or customize the behavior of an existing prebuilt or custom parser.
It enables extracting additional fields from raw logs without modifying the base parser.


To get more information about ParserExtension, see:

* [API documentation](https://cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.instances.logTypes.parserExtensions)
* How-to Guides
    * [Custom parsers overview](https://cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/detection/custom-parsers-overview)

## Example Usage - Chronicle Parserextension Basic


```hcl
resource "google_chronicle_parser_extension" "example" {
  location = "us"
  instance = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
  log_type = "CISCO_DHCP" # Unique log type to prevent CI parallel test collisions
  validation_skipped = true
  cbn_snippet = "ZHVtbXkgZXh0ZW5zaW9uIHNuaXBwZXQ=" # Base64 encoded "dummy extension snippet"
}
```
## Example Usage - Chronicle Parserextension Full


```hcl
resource "google_chronicle_parser_extension" "example" {
  location = "us"
  instance = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
  log_type = "AKAMAI_DHCP" # Unique log type to prevent CI parallel test collisions
  validation_skipped = true
  log = "ZHVtbXkgbG9n"
  
  field_extractors {
    log_format             = "JSON"
    append_repeated_fields = true

    preprocess_config {
      grok_regex = "(?P<message>.*)"
      target     = "message"
    }

    extractors {
      field_path       = "$.user"
      destination_path = "udm.principal.user.userid"
      value            = "static-override"
    }

    extractors {
      field_path         = "$.event"
      destination_path   = "udm.metadata.event_type"
      precondition_op    = "EQUALS"
      precondition_path  = "$.event"
      precondition_value = "login"
    }
  }
}
```
## Example Usage - Chronicle Parserextension Dynamic


```hcl
resource "google_chronicle_parser_extension" "example" {
  location = "us"
  instance = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
  log_type = "AKAMAI_DHCP" # Unique log type to prevent CI parallel test collisions
  validation_skipped = true
  log = "ZHVtbXkgbG9n"

  dynamic_parsing {
    opted_fields {
      path         = "$.ip"
      sample_value = "1.1.1.1"
    }
  }
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `location` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

* `instance` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

* `log_type` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.


* `cbn_snippet` -
  (Optional)
  Parser config could be a cbn snippet.

* `dynamic_parsing` -
  (Optional)
  A representation of a parser extension as dynamic parsing config.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_dynamic_parsing).

* `field_extractors` -
  (Optional)
  A representation of a parser extension as a set of field extractors.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_field_extractors).

* `log` -
  (Optional)
  Raw log used to assist the user in creation of augmentation.

* `validation_skipped` -
  (Optional)
  Flag to bypass parser extension validation.
  If enabled, the parser extension won't be rejected during the validation
  phase and validation will be skipped.

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

* `deletion_policy` - (Optional) Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the resource. Defaults to DELETE.
	When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the resource,
	the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
	When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
	management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
	When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.


<a name="nested_dynamic_parsing"></a>The `dynamic_parsing` block supports:

* `opted_fields` -
  (Optional)
  List of fields to be parsed.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_dynamic_parsing_opted_fields).


<a name="nested_dynamic_parsing_opted_fields"></a>The `opted_fields` block supports:

* `path` -
  (Optional)
  Path of the log field.

* `sample_value` -
  (Optional)
  Sample value of the log field.

<a name="nested_field_extractors"></a>The `field_extractors` block supports:

* `append_repeated_fields` -
  (Optional)
  Whether to append repeated fields or not.
  When false, repeated fields will be replaced.

* `extractors` -
  (Optional)
  List of FieldExtractors.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_field_extractors_extractors).

* `log_format` -
  (Optional)
  Possible values:
  JSON
  CSV
  XML

* `preprocess_config` -
  (Optional)
  PreProcessConfig holds the GROK expression to extract the syslog header.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_field_extractors_preprocess_config).

* `transformed_cbn_snippet` -
  (Output)
  CBN snippet generated from field extractors.


<a name="nested_field_extractors_extractors"></a>The `extractors` block supports:

* `destination_path` -
  (Optional)
  Path in generated event which is to be populated. This is required if the
  FieldExtractor is used to specify the parser extension.

* `field_path` -
  (Optional)
  Field path could be a json path, xml path or csv column name
  depending on log format. It refers to a section or substring in raw log.
  This is required if the FieldExtractor is used to specify the parser
  extension.

* `precondition_op` -
  (Optional)
  Operator used for precondition.
  Possible values:
  EQUALS
  NOT_EQUALS

* `precondition_path` -
  (Optional)
  Precondition path could be a json path, xml path or csv column name
  depending on log format. It refers to a section or substring in raw log.

* `precondition_value` -
  (Optional)
  Precondition value.

* `value` -
  (Optional)
  Value to be mapped to the destination path directly.

<a name="nested_field_extractors_preprocess_config"></a>The `preprocess_config` block supports:

* `grok_regex` -
  (Optional)
  GROK Regex to extract the structured part of the log.
  syntax documentation:
  www.elastic.co/guide/en/logstash/current/plugins-filters-grok.html

* `target` -
  (Optional)
  Target field name for the structured part of the log.
  This should match a SEMANTIC identifier from the grok expression.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/instances/{{instance}}/logTypes/{{log_type}}/parserExtensions/{{parserextension}}`

* `create_time` -
  The time the parser extension was created.

* `extension_validation_report` -
  The latest extension
  validation report for this extension.

* `last_live_time` -
  The time the config was last serving live traffic.

* `name` -
  Format:
  projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/logTypes/{logtype}/parserExtensions/{parserExtension}

* `state` -
  The state of the parser extension
  Possible values:
  NEW
  VALIDATING
  LIVE
  REJECTED
  INTERNAL_ERROR
  VALIDATED
  ARCHIVED
  VALIDATION_SKIPPED

* `state_last_changed_time` -
  The time the config state was last changed.

* `validation_report` -
  The validation report generated during extension validation.

* `parserextension` -
  Output only. The server-generated ID of the parser extension.


## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 20 minutes.

## Import


ParserExtension can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/instances/{{instance}}/logTypes/{{log_type}}/parserExtensions/{{parserextension}}`
* `{{project}}/{{location}}/{{instance}}/{{log_type}}/{{parserextension}}`
* `{{location}}/{{instance}}/{{log_type}}/{{parserextension}}`

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, use an [`identity` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/block/import#identity) to import ParserExtension using identity values. For example:

```tf
import {
  identity = {
    location = "<-required value->"
    instance = "<-required value->"
    log_type = "<-required value->"
    parserextension = "<-optional value->"
    project = "<-optional value->"
  }
  to = google_chronicle_parser_extension.default
}
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import ParserExtension using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/instances/{{instance}}/logTypes/{{log_type}}/parserExtensions/{{parserextension}}"
  to = google_chronicle_parser_extension.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), ParserExtension can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_chronicle_parser_extension.default projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/instances/{{instance}}/logTypes/{{log_type}}/parserExtensions/{{parserextension}}
$ terraform import google_chronicle_parser_extension.default {{project}}/{{location}}/{{instance}}/{{log_type}}/{{parserextension}}
$ terraform import google_chronicle_parser_extension.default {{location}}/{{instance}}/{{log_type}}/{{parserextension}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
